name: Hydrogen
description: Hydrogen is the lightest element and the most abundant chemical substance in the universe.

name: Helium
description: Helium is the second lightest and second most abundant element in the observable universe.

name: Lithium
description: Lithium is the lightest metal and the least dense solid element.

name: Beryllium
description: Beryllium is a hard, lightweight metal used in aerospace and nuclear applications.

name: Boron
description: Boron is a metalloid with important industrial and biological roles.

name: Carbon
description: Carbon is the basis of all known life on Earth and the fourth most abundant element in the universe.

name: Nitrogen
description: Nitrogen makes up 78% of Earth's atmosphere by volume.

name: Oxygen
description: Oxygen is essential for aerobic respiration in most living organisms.

name: Fluorine
description: Fluorine is the most electronegative element and most reactive nonmetal.

name: Neon
description: Neon glows red-orange in neon signs and is the fifth most abundant element in the universe.

name: Sodium
description: Sodium is a highly reactive metal essential to human health and widely used industrially.

name: Magnesium
description: Magnesium is the eighth most abundant element in the Earth's crust.

name: Aluminium
description: Aluminium is the most abundant metal in Earth's crust and widely used in manufacturing.

name: Silicon
description: Silicon is the backbone of modern electronics and the semiconductor industry.

name: Phosphorus
description: Phosphorus is essential for life, found in DNA, RNA, and ATP.

name: Sulfur
description: Sulfur is a bright yellow crystalline solid at room temperature.

name: Chlorine
description: Chlorine is a toxic gas used in water purification and as a disinfectant.

name: Argon
description: Argon is the third most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere.

name: Potassium
description: Potassium is vital for cell function and is the seventh most abundant element in Earth's crust.

name: Calcium
description: Calcium is essential for living organisms, particularly for building bones and teeth.

name: Scandium
description: Scandium is a rare earth metal used in aerospace and sports equipment.

name: Titanium
description: Titanium is a strong, lightweight metal with excellent corrosion resistance.

name: Vanadium
description: Vanadium is used to make steel alloys stronger and more wear-resistant.

name: Chromium
description: Chromium gives stainless steel its corrosion resistance and is used in chrome plating.

name: Manganese
description: Manganese is essential for steel production and plays a role in human metabolism.

name: Iron
description: Iron is the most used metal in the world and the primary component of steel.

name: Cobalt
description: Cobalt is used in superalloys, batteries, and gives glass a distinctive blue color.

name: Nickel
description: Nickel is used in stainless steel, batteries, and coins worldwide.

name: Copper
description: Copper is one of the few metals that occur naturally as an uncombined mineral.

name: Zinc
description: Zinc is essential for life and widely used for galvanizing steel to prevent rust.

name: Gallium
description: Gallium melts just above room temperature and is used in semiconductors and LEDs.

name: Germanium
description: Germanium is a lustrous metalloid used in fiber optics and infrared optics.

name: Arsenic
description: Arsenic is a notorious poison historically used in pesticides and formerly in medicine.

name: Selenium
description: Selenium is essential in trace amounts for many organisms and used in solar cells.

name: Bromine
description: Bromine is the only nonmetallic element that is a liquid at room temperature.

name: Krypton
description: Krypton is used in high-powered photographic flash lamps and is the fictional home planet of Superman.

name: Rubidium
description: Rubidium is a highly reactive alkali metal used in atomic clocks.

name: Strontium
description: Strontium is used in fireworks to produce red flames and in television picture tubes.

name: Yttrium
description: Yttrium is used in making phosphors for TV screens and LEDs.

name: Zirconium
description: Zirconium is highly resistant to corrosion and used in nuclear reactors.

name: Niobium
description: Niobium is used in superalloys for jet engines and in superconducting magnets.

name: Molybdenum
description: Molybdenum is essential for nitrogen fixation in plants and used in high-strength steel.

name: Technetium
description: Technetium was the first artificially produced element and has no stable isotopes.

name: Ruthenium
description: Ruthenium is a rare platinum-group metal used in catalysis and electronics.

name: Rhodium
description: Rhodium is the rarest and most valuable precious metal, used in catalytic converters.

name: Palladium
description: Palladium is used in catalytic converters and hydrogen storage.

name: Silver
description: Silver is the best conductor of electricity among all metals and has been used in currency for millennia.

name: Cadmium
description: Cadmium is used in rechargeable batteries and historically in pigments.

name: Indium
description: Indium is used in LCD screens and touchscreens as a transparent conductor.

name: Tin
description: Tin resists corrosion and is used to coat other metals and in solder.

name: Antimony
description: Antimony is a metalloid used in flame-retardant materials and semiconductors.

name: Tellurium
description: Tellurium is used in solar panels and to improve the machinability of copper and steel.

name: Iodine
appearance: Lustrous metallic gray, violet as gas
description: Iodine is essential for thyroid function and used as an antiseptic.

name: Xenon
description: Xenon is used in high-intensity arc lamps and ion propulsion systems for spacecraft.

name: Caesium
description: Caesium is used in atomic clocks which are the world's most accurate timekeepers.

name: Barium
description: Barium is used in X-ray imaging of the stomach and in green fireworks.

name: Lanthanum
description: Lanthanum is used in camera and telescope lenses and in rechargeable batteries.

name: Cerium
description: Cerium is the most abundant rare earth element and used in catalytic converters.

name: Praseodymium
description: Praseodymium is used in powerful magnets and in yellow glass coloring.

name: Neodymium
description: Neodymium magnets are the strongest permanent magnets known.

name: Promethium
description: Promethium is a radioactive element with no stable isotopes, used in pacemakers.

name: Samarium
description: Samarium-cobalt magnets are used in high-temperature environments.

name: Europium
description: Europium is used in phosphors for fluorescent lamps and TV screens.

name: Gadolinium
description: Gadolinium is used as a contrast agent in MRI scans.

name: Terbium
description: Terbium is used in solid-state devices and as a dopant in phosphors.

name: Dysprosium
description: Dysprosium is used in neodymium magnets to improve performance at higher temperatures.

name: Holmium
description: Holmium has the highest magnetic moment of any element and is used in lasers.

name: Erbium
description: Erbium-doped fiber amplifiers are essential in fiber optic communications.

name: Thulium
description: Thulium is used in portable X-ray devices and surgical lasers.

name: Ytterbium
description: Ytterbium is used in atomic clocks and as an alloying agent in stainless steel.

name: Lutetium
description: Lutetium is used in PET scan detectors and is the last of the lanthanides.

name: Hafnium
description: Hafnium is used in nuclear reactors as control rod material due to its ability to absorb neutrons.

name: Tantalum
description: Tantalum is highly corrosion-resistant and used in electronic capacitors.

name: Tungsten
description: Tungsten has the highest melting point of all metals at 3422°C.

name: Rhenium
description: Rhenium has the second highest melting point and is used in jet engine alloys.

name: Osmium
description: Osmium is the densest naturally occurring element on Earth.

name: Iridium
description: Iridium is the most corrosion-resistant metal and used in spark plugs.

name: Platinum
description: Platinum is used in catalytic converters, jewelry, and cancer chemotherapy.

name: Gold
description: Gold is one of the least reactive elements and has been prized throughout human history.

name: Mercury
description: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature and was historically used in thermometers.

name: Thallium
description: Thallium is a highly toxic metal once used in rat poison.

name: Lead
description: Lead is a soft, malleable metal with a long history of use, now known to be toxic.

name: Bismuth
description: Bismuth forms beautiful rainbow-colored crystals due to oxide layers.

name: Polonium
description: Polonium was discovered by Marie Curie and named after her homeland Poland.

name: Astatine
description: Astatine is the rarest naturally occurring element, with less than 1 gram on Earth at any time.

name: Radon
description: Radon is a radioactive gas that can accumulate in buildings and is a leading cause of lung cancer.

name: Francium
description: Francium is the second-rarest naturally occurring element and the most unstable of the naturally occurring elements.

name: Radium
description: Radium was discovered by Marie Curie and was once used in luminous paint on watch dials.

name: Actinium
description: Actinium was the first non-primordial radioactive element to be discovered.

name: Thorium
description: Thorium is a radioactive metal being researched as a safer nuclear fuel.

name: Protactinium
description: Protactinium is one of the rarest and most expensive naturally occurring elements.

name: Uranium
description: Uranium is used as fuel in nuclear power plants and was used in the first atomic bombs.

name: Neptunium
description: Neptunium was the first synthetic transuranium element to be produced.

name: Plutonium
description: Plutonium is used in nuclear weapons and as fuel in some nuclear reactors.

name: Americium
description: Americium is used in smoke detectors in homes worldwide.

name: Curium
description: Curium was named after Marie and Pierre Curie and glows bright red from its radioactivity.

name: Berkelium
description: Berkelium is named after Berkeley, California, where it was first synthesized.

name: Californium
description: Californium is used as a neutron source for nuclear reactors and cancer treatment.

name: Einsteinium
description: Einsteinium was named after Albert Einstein and first discovered in nuclear test debris.

name: Fermium
description: Fermium was named after Enrico Fermi and has no known uses outside of research.

name: Mendelevium
description: Mendelevium was named after Dmitri Mendeleev, creator of the periodic table.

name: Nobelium
description: Nobelium was named after Alfred Nobel, founder of the Nobel Prizes.

name: Lawrencium
description: Lawrencium is the last actinide and was named after Ernest Lawrence, inventor of the cyclotron.

name: Rutherfordium
description: Rutherfordium was named after Ernest Rutherford, discoverer of the atomic nucleus.

name: Dubnium
description: Dubnium is a synthetic element named after Dubna, Russia, where it was first synthesized.

name: Seaborgium
description: Seaborgium was named after Glenn T. Seaborg, who helped discover many transuranium elements.

name: Bohrium
description: Bohrium was named after Niels Bohr, pioneer of quantum mechanics and atomic structure.

name: Hassium
description: Hassium was named after the German state of Hesse, where it was discovered.

name: Meitnerium
description: Meitnerium was named after Lise Meitner, physicist who co-discovered nuclear fission.

name: Darmstadtium
description: Darmstadtium was named after Darmstadt, Germany, where it was first synthesized.

name: Roentgenium
description: Roentgenium was named after Wilhelm Röntgen, discoverer of X-rays.

name: Copernicium
description: Copernicium was named after Nicolaus Copernicus, founder of the heliocentric model.

name: Nihonium
description: Nihonium was the first element discovered in Asia, named from 'Nihon', the Japanese word for Japan.

name: Flerovium
description: Flerovium was named after the Flerov Laboratory of Nuclear Reactions in Russia.

name: Moscovium
description: Moscovium was named after the Moscow Oblast in Russia, where it was first produced.

name: Livermorium
description: Livermorium was named after Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in California.

name: Tennessine
description: Tennessine was named after Tennessee, home to Oak Ridge National Laboratory.

name: Oganesson
description: Oganesson was named after nuclear physicist Yuri Oganessian, the only living person with an element named after them.
